title: Android App Analysis - Pre-Refactor
objective: Analyze GeminiLiveDemo Android app (70+ Kotlin files) for refactor/implementation priorities
completed: Discovered Level 3 HCP already complete (seq 4-5): replay.ps1, recover.ps1, hcp-guard.ps1 all exist
Full build configuration audit: compileSdk=35, minSdk=26, AGP=8.8.0, Kotlin=2.0.0, Gradle=9.4.1
39 unit tests all pass (Phase5UnitTest, SessionStateHolderTest, SituationalArchitectureTest, SpeakerLockManagerTest)
Dependency tree resolved with no conflicts
Identified 6 categories of lint errors (30 total errors, 339 warnings)
Identified missing Gemini API key in local.properties (runtime failure for Gemini features)
Identified JAVA_HOME not set in environment
Catalogued all 55+ Kotlin source files across 14 packages
Architecture mapped: 6 layers (Network, Audio, Service, Situational, Memory, Multi-Agent Dating)
pending: Fix 30 lint errors: VIBRATE permission (2), getITelephony reflection (1), TileService deprecated API (2), RECEIVER_EXPORTED flag (1), android:tint → app:tint in XML (23), uses-feature telephony (1)
Set up API keys: add gemini.api.key and deepgram.api.key to local.properties with BuildConfig injection
Consider refactoring FloatingWidgetService (3000+ lines) into smaller components
Set JAVA_HOME permanently via environment variable
Add proguard rules for kotlinx.serialization, SQLCipher, and Google API client

agents.name: AI Agent (opencode)
agents.responsibility: Analyze and refactor GeminiLiveDemo Android app

decisions.decision: Prioritize fixing 30 lint errors + API key setup before feature work
decisions.rationale: Lint failures block CI/CD; missing API key makes Gemini features unusable at runtime

knowledge: HCP Level 3 fully implemented: 6 scripts (checkpoint, resume, audit, replay, recover, hcp-guard) + docs
Project uses Gemini 3.1 Flash Live Preview (not 3.5 Flash for real-time)
App uses Java-WebSocket library (not OkHttp WebSocket) for Gemini Live API connection
Deepgram API key is hardcoded in GeminiMeetingService.kt — should be moved to BuildConfig
SQLCipher 4.5.4 for encrypted databases; 3 separate DBs (AssistantMemory, Meetings, DateProfiles)
FloatingWidgetService.kt is the largest file at ~3000 lines handling session lifecycle, audio, sensor coordination

assumptions.assumption: lint errors are the primary blocker for production readiness
JAVA_HOME pointing to Android Studio JBR is sufficient for all builds

risks.risk: getITelephony reflection will break on API 35+ (already targeting SDK 35)
Missing Gemini API key makes all Gemini features fail silently
risks.mitigation: Add key validation at app startup with user-facing error

risks.risk: Deepgram key hardcoded in source code — security leak if pushed to public repo
risks.mitigation: Move to BuildConfig injected from local.properties

next_steps: Fix 30 lint errors (starting with highest impact: getITelephony reflection, VIBRATE permission, RECEIVER_EXPORTED flag)
Instruct user to add gemini.api.key + deepgram.api.key to local.properties
Move hardcoded Deepgram key from GeminiMeetingService.kt to BuildConfig
Run gradlew check again to verify lint fixes

open_questions.question: Should FloatingWidgetService be split into multiple smaller services or remains as-is?
Should we add CI/CD (GitHub Actions) for auto lint + test?

context_summary: HCP Level 3 complete with all 6 scripts. Android app compiles and tests pass, but has 30 lint errors + missing API keys. Starting with lint fixes and API key setup before deeper refactoring.
